entries.title: Building an Adversarial Consensus Engine | Multi-Agent LLMs for Automated Malware Analysis
entries.summary: Single-tool LLM analysis produces reports that look authoritative but aren't. A serial consensus pipeline catches artifacts and hallucinations at source.

entries.title: LABScon25 Replay | Your Apps May Be Gone, But the Hackers Made $9 Billion and They’re Still Here
entries.summary: Andrew MacPherson exposes how crypto thieves exploit DeFi architecture, from the $1.5 billion Bybit heist to drainers-as-a-service and fund laundering.

entries.title: From Narrative to Knowledge Graph | LLM-Driven Information Extraction in Cyber Threat Intelligence
entries.summary: LLMs can turn CTI narratives into structured intelligence at scale, but speed-accuracy trade-offs demand careful design for operational defense workflows.

entries.title: Silent Brothers | Ollama Hosts Form Anonymous AI Network Beyond Platform Guardrails
entries.summary: Analysis of 175,000 open-source AI hosts across 130 countries reveals a vast compute layer susceptible to resource hijacking and code execution attacks.

entries.title: LABScon25 Replay | How to Bug Hotel Rooms v2.0
entries.summary: Dan Tentler reveals how consumer hardware coupled with Home Assistant can monitor hotel rooms, detect occupants through walls, and trigger automated alerts.

entries.title: LLMs in the SOC (Part 1) | Why Benchmarks Fail Security Operations Teams
entries.summary: LLM cybersecurity benchmarks fail to measure what defenders need: faster detection, reduced containment time, and better decisions under pressure.

entries.title: LABScon25 Replay | Hacktivism and War: A Clarifying Discussion
entries.summary: Jim Walter unpacks the hacktivist landscape and reveals how to distinguish different levels of threat based on persona characteristics.

entries.title: Inside the LLM | Understanding AI & the Mechanics of Modern Attacks
entries.summary: Learn how attackers exploit tokenization, embeddings and LLM attention mechanisms to bypass LLM security filters and hijack model behavior.

entries.title: LLMs & Ransomware | An Operational Accelerator, Not a Revolution
entries.summary: LLMs make competent ransomware crews faster and novices more dangerous. The risk is not superintelligent malware, but rather industrialized extortion.

entries.title: Malicious Apprentice | How Two Hackers Went From Cisco Academy to Cisco CVEs
entries.summary: Read how two Cisco Network Academy Cup winners went from students to operators behind Salt Typhoon, a global cyber espionage campaign targeting telecoms.
